title: Retro Sounds and Textures
description: A pack that brings back some pre-1.17 and 1.16 things.
body:
This is a resource pack meant to recreate the sounds, looks and feel of older versions of the game in some ways. What this pack does is:

- Remove all ambient Nether sounds
- Replaces netherrack, netherbrick, basalt, tuff, and tuff brick sounds with regular stone sounds
- Replaces crimson and warped wart and stem sounds with leaf and log sounds, respectively
- Retextures the raw iron, gold, and copper item textures to look a bit better
- Replaces all ore textures to look like their older counterparts (deepslate too)
- Removes the Java Edition logo
- Restores the older, more pixelated Minecraft logo
- Restores the pre-1.13 panorama to the original

For the closest-to-retro experience possible, I recommend combining this pack with my other pack, [No New Music](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/texture-packs/no-new-music), and Programmer Art. Here's the ideal pack layout:
![A resource pack list in Minecraft. This pack being at the top, followed by No New Music and Programmer Art.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ba0b488a4faa8187b27fdce3385648f5e8a49f3c.png)


This is what the menu looks like with the recommend pack-stack:
![Minecraft's main menu.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9049ba1206fe0b007b8fb48f5e86e54a7dc4011a.png)


And here are the retextured ores:
![A screenshot displaying the custom raw ore textures this pack provides.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/fc6c770c2ada606d4e053f0a87c99a51fc9df829.png)


Logo, panorama, and button tweaks were modified using [VanillaTweaks](https://vanillatweaks.net/).

Sounds modified by yours truly, AgentLoneStar007.

Ore retextures were done by Ravencross15.

_And before you ask, no, I'm not on Windows 7. I use Linux, and this is a skin for it._

If you wish to use this resource pack in a modpack, feel free to. Just be sure to link it in your project page.
